title: Midnight Craving Russian Dough Pierogi
description: A bouncy take on traditional Russian pierogi, perfect for a midnight craving. The combination of dark brown soft sugar and pressure-cooked dough creates a unique Mediterranean-friendly dish.

ingredients.name: all-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: use a high-protein flour for best results

ingredients.name: warm water
ingredients.notes: use a thermometer to ensure water is at 100°F (38°C) for best yeast activation

ingredients.name: dark brown soft sugar
ingredients.notes: do not skip, as this is a key ingredient

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: use kosher salt for best flavor

ingredients.name: active dry yeast
ingredients.notes: use a high-quality yeast for best results

ingredients.name: unsalted butter
ingredients.notes: use softened butter for best results

ingredients.name: egg
ingredients.notes: use a large egg for best results

ingredients.name: ricotta cheese
ingredients.notes: use whole-milk ricotta for best flavor

instructions: Combine flour, warm water, dark brown soft sugar, and salt in a large mixing bowl. Mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes, until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until it has doubled in size.
Divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces.
Roll out each piece into a thin circle.
Place a tablespoon of ricotta cheese in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the ricotta cheese to form a half-circle shape, and press the edges together to seal the pierogi.
Bring a large pot of water to a boil, and add 1 tablespoon of salt.
Gently add the pierogi to the pot, and cook for 5-7 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
Remove the pierogi from the pot with a slotted spoon, and serve hot.
Melt 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add 1 tablespoon of dark brown soft sugar to the melted butter, and whisk until combined.
Pour the sugar-butter sauce over the cooked pierogi, and serve immediately.
